links.targetTitle: PSS provides 1-to-1 asymmetrically encrypted messaging with 2–10 second latency on Swarm.
PSS provides 1-to-1 asymmetrically encrypted messaging with 2–10 second latency on Swarm.

global.title: Public profile feed publishes the beeNodePublicKey and overlayAddress that PSS and ACT need.
global.content:
## Schema

Each user publishes a public profile at feed `ph:v2:profile:<address>`:

```typescript
{
  address: "0xadbA7C2F...",       // Swarm signer address
  ethAddress: "0x...",            // Original ETH wallet (optional)
  beeNodePublicKey: "02abc...",   // Compressed secp256k1 — for PSS targeting
  swarmPublicKey: "03def...",     // Wallet-derived — for ECDH key agreement
  overlayAddress: "abc123...",    // Bee overlay — for PSS/GSOC addressing
  updatedAt: "2026-04-15T..."
}
```

## Role in chat/collab

- `beeNodePublicKey` is the PSS recipient key. It is also the ACT grantee pubkey.
- `overlayAddress` is the PSS target and the GSOC-signer mining target.
- `swarmPublicKey` is the ECDH partner for wallet-level key agreement.
- `ethAddress` enables ENS → profile lookup (ENS resolves to ETH address, and profile indexes by ETH address).

## Why unencrypted

The profile feed is intentionally **unencrypted** — it serves as a user-discovery directory. Discovery without some public rendezvous point is impossible; the data here is deliberately non-sensitive (public keys and overlay addresses, not wallet private keys or personal content).
global.description: Feed ph:v2:profile:<address> exposes beeNodePublicKey for PSS encryption targeting and overlayAddress for PSS/GSOC neighborhood routing — the per-user directory for chat.
